tokens: Can you put that in that sampler tool bag, there?

tokens: Okay, Bob, the battery covers are closed.

tokens: Okay, I've got the (SEP) transmitter (as per

tokens: Okay, and I copy...(Pause) Okay, torque to 279 will be the heading.

tokens: I'm waiting for my minute and a half here.

tokens: I'm at the SEP site (planned to be 100 meters east of the LM), and I found a place I think we can lay out a pretty good grid.

tokens: Okay, Jack, and when you lay it (the transmitter) down there, we want to put it down with the gnomon side, the side you're going to face the Sun, you want to put that facing away from the Sun.

transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gnomon...You want the gnomon side or corner?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The gnomon side away from the Sun.
The side with the solar panels on it (has) to be in the shade.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, everything's working fine so far.
And I'm ready to press on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I'm facing the other way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I tell you, just about all you can see in that direction (east) is the LM.

transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Go right to the LM, and...or, a little bit to your left, to the left (north) of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I've got to go to the LM and give them a reading here (of range and bearing to the ALSEP site).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Making a general comment to Jack) You get that shadow (of the LM) up there (to block the glare of the Sun) and you're all right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We want the range and bearing at the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yes, sir, I'll give it to you.
I even got...(To himself, coming up on a big hole) Ooh!

I don't know if you're using it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we're using the LM right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, everything I've seen so far indicates that the so-called "subfloor" boulders - if we have gotten that deep - are this gabbro.

transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, let me give them a range.
I'll be on my way out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm standing (parked) right in front of the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The zap pits have nice white halos, although, for the most part, the rock's too coarse to show them very well.
Some of the larger ones have white halos.
(Pause) We may not be down to the subfloor, but it's hard to say.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, making 8 to 10 kilometers (per hour), and I'm barely moving.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Where've you got the SEP, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me give them a bearing, distance, and range, and some numbers here (at the SEP transmitter).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I did see a dense gray rock that's different than the others on my traverse out here.
We'll try to find some of that, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm reading 278, 003, and 003 at the SEP site.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And how about giving me amp-hours and batteries just as long as you're there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yes, sir, it's coming at you.
Amp-hours are 112 and 110; batteries are 92 (degrees) and about 112 (degrees).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're going to go to Reset, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, you can be getting on.
You won't need a bomb (seismic charge), and I guess you won't need the LMP camera unless you want it.
We'll be deploying the bomb at Steno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Laughing) I thought we were playing it by the checklist, Geno.

transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, it just happens that the station is at the place we were going to deploy the charge.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, well, we got it off (the transporter on the back of the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you know which side of Steno he wants us to go, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me fill you in on the plan, guys.

We're going to go to the west side of Steno, which is where you would have driven by anyway, and the stop will be at the 340/1.2, which is where you've got the little Delta for EP 6, in your checklist.
And we will plan on spending about three-zero minutes there sampling, primarily boulders.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Geno, west side of Steno, then.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got it here (in the checklist).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got a good feeling on how to head out of here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I want to get around the back side - now that I'm down there - on the back side of Trident, and make sure that what I'm looking at is Trident over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me try to get on this thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17, just to fill you in a little bit more here.
We're looking at a six (hour) plus four-five (minute) EVA.
We've given you fifteen minutes to drive to Station 1; thirty minutes at Station 1; and fifteen minutes to drive back to the (spelling the acronym) S.E.P., and then deploying the S.E.P.
And then a four-zero-minute close-out at six plus four five.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: After 30 minutes at Station 1, what did you say?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Then we're going to drive back.
There's a 15-minute return to the SEP site; and then 22 minutes at the SEP site to deploy the SEP; and then return to the LM and 45 minutes for the close-out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, we've got to start getting in this Rover facing 90 degrees to the seats, I think.

tokens: I tried to knock it all off my feet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if we can't get around Trident East over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I wish I didn't have this charge.

tokens: Okay, this is Trident, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: So, you're starting out on the...You really want to head about 29...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 17, we'll start out on the same general traverse that you've been on.

transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're just getting our bearings, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This (possibly Poppie) has got to be Trident East, right here, Jack.

tokens: On the right or the left?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Poppie was just over about where...

tokens: I just want to get our bearings here.

tokens: It (the map) says (to) go along this way.

tokens: Ho - ho - ho - hold it, hold it, hold it!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Got it, got it, got it (garbled).

transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to head about one two zero (120 degrees) out of here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got another hole on your right here.

tokens: I'm not sure what's the matter.
Why don't you go left there?

tokens: For your advice, we're trying to use the low-gain antenna on this traverse also.

tokens: Gene, I think we need to head south.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've got to go out here southeast.

tokens: You mean the...(Pause) I'm not sure I can get to it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's calling for 116 at 0.6 to (garbled) near the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I ended up with this charge in my hand.

transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think you're getting...That must be Emory over there.

tokens: This is very easily Steno right over here.
Let's see, we're between the two big ones...That would be...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That would be Powell on the right.

tokens: Certainly doesn't look like the L&A (Landing and Ascent facility) yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about a range and bearing, guys, I think we can help you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, it sounds like you're probably just driving by the East Trident or Trident 3.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You think all that right there is Trident?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: My god, if it is, that's incredible.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there...You're going to go in a hole with your right...No problem.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see the lip too well because of the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, do you have your camera on...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, I wish they'd caught me with this (charge before I got on the Rover)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...If so, could you give me a frame count some time?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I got my hands full with this charge.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, okay, forgot about that one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, you know that is...Don't you suppose that's Trident?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, it sure looks like it, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We were quite a ways from Trident.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's about right; we're half a mile (means "kilometer")...That's about right.
Boy, what I was looking at (as) Trident isn't anywhere near that big.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, if that's true, then we want to go...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yes, sir, we're all right now.
What we were looking at before...I've got to stop and see what that is.
I've got to look at those maps when we get in (the LM after the EVA).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, it's a triplet all right, with some septum between.
(Pause) Well, wish I could take pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let me get a few here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We can get them coming back.
(Pause) (I can) take a few, but it's not continuous.
I wish I hadn't said "follow the checklist".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the surface has not really changed except slightly more hummocky and rolling, because of a larger number of irregular depressions or craters.
(They've hit a small crater.) The rocks at first glance from the Rover look very much like what we had around the LM.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, you might be expecting water flag and a tone in a couple of minutes, to go to Aux.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And CDR('s tone) will be about 5 minutes after that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll get stopped here in a minute, Jack; as soon as I get...(Listens) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think maybe that might be Steno over there...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think we're too far off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, there's my...I've got to go to Aux.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Houston, do you see me in Aux?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to hit some of these (craters) broadside, Jack, and then we won't get any roll angle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, how far have you come?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Then that's Steno with all the blocks in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, am I glad we didn't land out here!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See this high point up here coming ahead?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That should give us our bearings, I hope.
(Pause) I can't hold that bomb any longer.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What are you going to do with it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to drop it at my feet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: My hands aren't going to be any good for sampling.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Long Pause while Jack looks at the map)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Okay, if that's Powell...Quite a ways over there, but I think the thing to do is get up on that little ridge there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah; I think we may end up looking right into Steno when we get up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 340 and one point...(Stops to listen to Gene)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Are you reading the low gain, by the way?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 340 and 1.2 is where we expect the station to be.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And it should be up on the top of a little bit of a rise.
That you see coming up there.
You ought to be in the vicinity of some very large boulders.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Houston, (pause as he listens to Bob) there are certainly a lot of big boulders...Whoop!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me take a look into the Sun (east) here.
That doesn't look what I thought Steno looked like.
This is it over here, though, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, Steno ought to be right at your 9 o'clock there, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I forgot which one it is.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Bob) How do you know where we are?
I think you're probably right, although it doesn't impress me as what I saw in the L&A (Landing and Ascent).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How much time have we got to drive now, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that's probably Emory up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene and Jack, we'd like you to...If you're in the vicinity, we think you're just about there.
We were planning on you leaving the SEP and getting to this place at about 4 plus 58 (since depressurization) and we're showing about 5 plus 00 right now so you're right on time.
And if you're at 340 and 1.2 in that vicinity, you must be at the station or very close to it where you can see.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, it doesn't look real familiar, Bob, as far as Steno's concerned.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I got (my tone and water flag)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think they can locate us if we work that block field right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, on the map, Jack, that you're probably looking at, you're seeing that, with north being 12 o'clock, there are a couple of boulders at about the 09:30 position on Steno.
And then there's a couple of more at about the 9 o'clock position on Steno.
And we're putting the station right in the midst of all those boulders.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's hard to follow that that's where we are.
It doesn't look like what I expected Steno to look like.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What's the range and bearing one more time?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think it would almost be worth...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I bet that's Emory up on that hill.
(Pause; brief static as Gene turns) It's got to be.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We better park in this boulder field here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wish we could have gotten near one of the big ones (that is, near the big boulders Bob has been talking about), but let's do it.
We're going to run out of time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's no point in deviating around and spending 15 minutes trying to get a particular spot or down to a bigger boulder.
You must be in the near vicinity.
If you're really worried about it, I guess you might drive a little bit to the east to the rim of the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'm parked 180 (facing south as per
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Stand by on that a minute.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You want us to get off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was just wondering about where you were going to park.
There was a question on whether they wanted us to park into the Sun, but don't worry.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'm headed 182, 346 (bearing back to the LM), 1.2 (distance driven), 1.1 (range to the SEP transmitter), 110, 108 (amp-hours remaining on each of the two batteries), 100, and 118 (battery temperatures), and off-scale-low on all of the motors.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) You want this charge deployed here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can deploy it right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, the fenders are still on, thank goodness.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll give you the Taper of the Year award.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, you're going to have to give me the Duster of the Year Award after this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) That will be in the pans, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just to confirm that is EP-6, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob; we're about 15 meters from a 20-meter (diameter) blocky-rimmed crater.
All the blocks on the rim look like the pyroxene/plagioclase gabbro - the vesicular rock - (static) seen at the LM.
At least all that I've seen so far.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And is this crater to the east or west?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's to the northwest of the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
